{{Quote|Is it true you are pursuing Moff Sarne?|Dana Cadwell, to the ''FarStar'' delegation following their arrival on Gandle Ott|<ref name="dirp" />}}
'''Dana Cadwell''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Journalist/Legends|reporter]] on [[Gandle Ott]], a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. A noted figure on the planet, she worked primarily for the [[KatholNet]] [[NewsNet]], as well as being [[TriNebulon News/Legends|TriNebulon News]]' planetary correspondent. In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], Cadwell covered the arrival of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'' to Gandle Ott. The ''FarStar''—in [[DarkStryder Campaign|pursuit]] of the former [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] ruler of the Kathol [[Sector/Legends|sector]], [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]]—visited the planet for several [[Standard day/Legends|days]], engaging in [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] ventures that Cadwell reported on. It was her hope that the story would be big enough news to earn her a position away from Gandle Ott.

==Biography==
{{Quote|Are you here to annex Gandle Ott for the New Republic?|Dana Cadwell, to the ''FarStar'' delegation following their arrival on Gandle Ott|<ref name="dirp" />}}
Dana Cadwell was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Journalist/Legends|reporter]] based on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Gandle Ott]] in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. She worked for the [[NewsNet]] [[KatholNet]], as well as being the planetary correspondent for [[TriNebulon News/Legends|TriNebulon News]]. Cadwell was a popular personality, and influential enough to be invited to notable parties and events, but longed to leave Gandle Ott and break into a larger news bureau outside of the Kathol [[Sector/Legends|sector]].<ref name="dirp" />

In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]], [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] of the Kathol sector, was forced to [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|retreat]] from his [[Capital/Legends|capital]] on [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]] by [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[New Republic task force (Kal'Shebbol)|forces]]. Sarne fled to Gandle Ott, took on supplies and left with the majority of the planet's Imperial personnel, including the [[Unidentified Imperial Governor (Gandle Ott)|governor]] and [[Gandle Ott system defense force|defense force]]. The resulting power vacuum on Gandle Ott created confusion, with both [[Lieutenant governor/Legends|Vice-Governor]] [[Marja Lang]] and [[General/Legends|General]] [[Herron Dade]] vying for control of the remaining government. Cadwell instantly saw that Sarne's retreat and the arrival of the New Republic in the Kathol sector was just the story she needed to get the attention of TriNebulon News.<ref name="dirp" />

Shortly after Sarne's departure, the New Republic [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'' arrived in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] in [[DarkStryder Campaign|pursuit]] of Sarne. When the ''FarStar'' landed at [[Montrol Spaceport]], Cadwell was part of a large media presence that witnessed a delegation from the [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]] meet with Acting-Governor Lang, [[Mayor/Legends|Mayor]] [[Stanfeld Duffit]], and [[Olaver Lansel]], Gandle Ott's Imperial liaison. As the ''FarStar'' delegation progressed through the [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]], Cadwell attempted to learn of the ''FarStar''{{'s}} mission by asking several questions regarding Moff Sarne, the rumor that he had [[DarkStryder technology|exotic technology]], and the New Republic's intentions toward Gandle Ott. The next [[Standard day/Legends|day]], Cadwell covered the [[Ceremonial Mushroom Cutting]] presided over by [[General/Legends|General]] Herron Dade, where Dade effectively declared their independence from the Empire and encouraged Gandle Ott to take control of its own future once more.<ref name="dirp" />

==Personality and traits==
Dana Cadwell had [[Color/Legends|strawberry blonde]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]], dressed in smart, subdued clothing and always carried the equipment she needed about her person. Street-smart and savvy, she was well-connected in Gandle Ott society, doggedly determined, and an investigative reporter capable of uncovering facts and hidden secrets. Cadwell's only loyalty was to herself, and getting a story was her top priority. She had a working knowledge of the way bureaucracy worked and an interest in [[Star system/Legends|planetary systems]] and cultures. She could operate a [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] vehicle. Despite her status on Gandle Ott, Cadwell was desperate to attract the attention of a larger news agency and move to a planet closer to the center of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="dirp" />

==Behind the scenes==
Dana Cadwell was introduced in ''[[Death Is Remotely Possible]]'', the third [[Roleplaying game|RPG]] adventure in ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' published by [[West End Games]] in [[1995]]. Cadwell can be used by the gamemaster to either help or hinder the player characters during their [[Time/Legends|time]] on Gandle Ott. Since Cadwell is loyal to herself and her story above all else, she has no personal feelings either way about the ''FarStar''{{'s}} visit and her attitude towards the crew will be determined by the outcome of their first meeting. Cadwell can be used by the gamemaster to put the characters back on track, to get them out of trouble, or to misdirect them completely. As both an investigative reporter and a figure in Gandle Ott society, Cadwell can conceivably appear at any point in the adventure.

Cadwell is mentioned in the following adventure, ''[[Shintel Downtime]]''; during the events in the scenario, the ''FarStar'' becomes [[Skirmish at Shintel|stranded]] in the [[Shintel system/Legends|Shintel system]]. Two options are available to the players; either to send their [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]]-equipped [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]] for help, or to cannibalize the X-wing's hyperdrives to repair the ''FarStar''{{'s}} engines temporarily and limp to the [[Tanquilla Beach/Legends|''Tanquilla Beach'']] [[Shadowport/Legends|shadowport]]. If the players chose to send their fighters back to Gandle Ott, then Cadwell accompanies a rescue [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]] back to [[Shintel]] to cover the story of how the New Republic [[Starship/Legends|starship]] became stranded and had to be assisted by Imperial forces. As ''Shintel Downtime'' assumes the players reach ''Tanquilla Beach'' by jury-rigging the engines, Cadwell's involvement in the adventure is presumed to have not happened.
